Q7 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

A 750 mW maximum power dissipation diode at 25°C has 6 mW/°C de-rating factor. If the forward voltage drop remains constant at 0.7 V, the maximum forward current at 73°C is _______ mA.
(Rounded upto nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 660
Solution

660 (658 - 662)

The average steady-state voltage across diode D2 (with polarity as mentioned) is _________ volt.
(Rounded upto nearest integer)
NAT
Correct answer: -9
Solution

Q9 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Select the correct option(s) regarding clipper circuits:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a, c)
• Clipping circuits are wave shaping circuits, used to clip off the portions of an input waveform.
They can be used to limit the amplitude of a waveform to a certain level, thus protecting
circuit from damage as a result of over-voltage condition.
• Clipper circuits can also be designed using op-amps.
• Clamper circuits are known as D.C. restorer as they adjust the DC level of the waveform.
Thus, options (a) and (c) are correct.
